hero.title: 9 Days, Kerala to Land's End.
hero.titleAccent: The Backwaters and the Southern Tip of India.
hero.intro: Travel the length of Kerala and on to Kanyakumari, the very tip of the Indian subcontinent, where three seas meet and you can watch the sun rise and set over the ocean from the same spot.
hero.heroImageAlt: Kanyakumari southern tip of India sunset

overview.heading: 9 Days Kerala + Kanyakumari: The Full Picture
body: Kanyakumari, at the southernmost tip of mainland India, is where the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal and the Indian Ocean meet. It is a place of pilgrimage and pilgrimage-worthy sunsets, home to the Vivekananda Rock Memorial and the towering Thiruvalluvar statue rising from the sea.

This 9 day package from Malaysia travels the length of Kerala, taking in Kochi, Munnar, the backwaters and the coast, before reaching Kanyakumari at land's end. Arranged privately with your own vehicle and driver guide, it is a journey from God's Own Country to the edge of the subcontinent.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Kanyakumari: Where Three Seas Meet
items.body: The southern tip of India, where you can watch sunrise and sunset over the ocean. The Vivekananda Rock Memorial and the Thiruvalluvar statue rise dramatically from the sea offshore.

items.title: Kerala Backwaters & Tea Hills
items.body: The journey south includes the classic Kerala experiences: Munnar's tea hills and an overnight houseboat on the backwaters.

items.title: Temple Coast & Padmanabhapuram
items.body: The route takes in the temple-rich coast around Trivandrum, including the magnificent Padmanabhaswamy Temple and the wooden palace of Padmanabhapuram.

days.title: Arrive Kochi
days.description: Met at Cochin airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the fishing nets.

days.title: Kochi to Munnar
days.description: Scenic drive to the tea hills.

days.title: Munnar Sightseeing
days.description: A full day among the tea estates and viewpoints.

days.title: Munnar to Alleppey Houseboat
days.description: Descend to the backwaters and board your private houseboat.

days.title: Alleppey to Trivandrum
days.description: Disembark and drive south to Trivandrum, Kerala's capital.

days.title: Trivandrum to Kanyakumari
days.description: Drive to Kanyakumari at the southern tip, via Padmanabhapuram Palace.

days.title: Kanyakumari Sightseeing
days.description: A full day at land's end.

days.title: Kanyakumari to Trivandrum, Beach
days.description: Return towards Trivandrum with a beach stop at Kovalam.

days.title: Depart Trivandrum
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Trivandrum airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Kerala to Land's End, Seamlessly Routed
whyBook.body: A journey this long, from Kochi to the tip of India, needs careful planning to flow well. We route it so each leg builds on the last. Dhesu has arranged South India journeys for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Kerala & Kanyakumari Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 9 day journey combines the best of Kerala with a trip to Kanyakumari, the southern tip of India. It is ideal for travellers who want to go beyond the usual Kerala loop and reach the dramatic meeting point of three seas.

The route takes in Munnar, the backwaters, the temple coast and Kanyakumari's iconic sunrise and sunset, all linked by a private vehicle and driver guide.

Kanyakumari adds a sense of pilgrimage and arrival, standing at the very edge of the subcontinent.

sections.heading: What to See in Kanyakumari
sections.body:
Kanyakumari is famous for its ocean sunrises and sunsets, visible from the same shore. Offshore, the Vivekananda Rock Memorial and the 41-metre Thiruvalluvar statue rise from the sea, reached by ferry.

The Kanyakumari Amman Temple and the meeting of the three seas give the town its spiritual significance. It is a fitting, dramatic end to a journey through the south.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Cochin or Trivandrum Airport (COK / TRV). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Can you really see sunrise and sunset over the sea at Kanyakumari?
items.answer: Yes. Because it sits at the southern tip where three seas meet, Kanyakumari is one of the few places in India where you can watch both sunrise and sunset over the ocean from the same area, weather permitting.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: September to March offers the most pleasant weather across Kerala and the southern coast. The monsoon brings heavy rain. The cool, dry season is best for the hills, backwaters and the Kanyakumari coast alike.

items.question: How much driving is involved over 9 days?
items.answer: This is a journey down the length of Kerala and into Tamil Nadu, so there are several scenic drives. The nine-day pace keeps each day comfortable, and the private vehicle makes the longer legs relaxing with stops along the way.
